body.title: queue_view._STATUS_LABEL mislabels akien as legacy — contradicts ownership-bucket decision
body.description:
lab/claudecode/queue_view.py:_STATUS_LABEL has `"akien": "Akien (legacy)"`, which contradicts the explicit decision (Akien 2026-06-16) that `akien` is his intentional at-a-glance "these are mine" ownership bucket, NOT a deprecated legacy gate. The ~/bin view tools (uuopentickets/uumytickets) were already corrected to render `"👤 Akien (yours)"`; queue_view.py is the remaining drift in the same regression family (consequence-checking gap — a label shipped before the keep-akien correction landed). Align the web /queue renderer's label with Akien's bucket semantics.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/queue_view.py (_STATUS_LABEL akien entry); confirm no other renderer still tags akien legacy. Reference: ~/bin/uuopentickets, ~/bin/uumytickets (already correct).
**Design rules:** test-plan; no-sqlite n/a.
**Scope boundary:** IN - change the akien label to Akien's ownership-bucket wording (e.g. "\ud83d\udc64 Akien (yours)") and move it out of the `# legacy:` comment block; add/adjust a test asserting the akien row renders the ownership label, not 'legacy'. OUT - touching other status labels, the status model, or the bin tools.
**Test plan:** add/extend a test in tests/test_web_queue.py asserting the akien-status row renders the ownership-bucket label and NOT the string 'legacy'; full tests/test_web_queue.py green.
body.result: DickSimnel.0: DONE: Aligned akien label with ownership-bucket semantics in queue_view.py
